questions.question: Which statement about acetaminophen (paracetamol) overdose is correct?
choices: Hepatic failure and jaundice appear immediately after ingestion
The Rumack-Matthew nomogram uses serum levels drawn ≥4 hours post-ingestion to determine the need for N-acetylcysteine (NAC)
Antidote therapy is unnecessary if more than 24 hours have passed since ingestion
Hemodialysis is the first-line antidotal treatment
questions.explanation: In acetaminophen overdose, patients are typically asymptomatic or have mild nausea/vomiting immediately after ingestion; hepatotoxicity usually manifests 24–72 hours later. The Rumack-Matthew nomogram uses serum acetaminophen levels drawn ≥4 hours after ingestion to determine NAC need. The antidote is NAC (not flumazenil, which reverses benzodiazepines). NAC should be continued even beyond 24 hours if hepatotoxicity is present.

questions.question: Select 3 signs that are characteristic of organophosphate poisoning.
questions.explanation: Organophosphates inhibit acetylcholinesterase, producing muscarinic effects (SLUDGE: salivation, lacrimation, urination, defecation, GI distress, emesis; plus miosis, increased bronchial secretions, bradycardia) and nicotinic effects (muscle fasciculations, weakness). Mydriasis, dry skin, and hyperthermia are signs of anticholinergic (atropine-type) toxidrome, not organophosphate poisoning. Treatment is high-dose atropine and early pralidoxime (2-PAM).
